proposal.reasoning: This is the initial exploration phase with no prior data, requiring broad sampling across parameter space to identify promising regions. I'm proposing 4 distinct physical hypotheses: (1) a balanced baseline with moderate parameters, (2) strong van der Waals cohesion to maximize density and heat of vaporization, (3) strong electrostatic character with high charges and extended dipole separation to target the dipole moment and surface tension, and (4) rigid linear geometry with stiff force constants to test conformational effects on packing. This diversity ensures we test the relative importance of dispersion vs. electrostatics vs. molecular shape in achieving the target properties (density 1.095, Hvap 12.6, ST 42, dipole 3.96). All values respect physical constraints: dummy bond lengths < sigma/2 (all < 1.5 while sigma/2 ≈ 2.5-2.7), dummy force constants > 100 kcal/mol/Å², and charges are equal and opposite.

hypothesis.scientific_rationale: We are testing four distinct physical regimes: Fork 1 (baseline) uses moderate cohesion (ε=-0.5) and polarity (q=±0.3) to establish a reference. Fork 2 (strong cohesion) increases |ε| to -0.7 and reduces σ to enhance London dispersion forces, expecting ↑Density and ↑Hvap. Fork 3 (high polarity) increases charges to ±0.45 and extends dummy bond lengths to 1.2Å to maximize dipole moment (μ=q×d), targeting the 3.96D target and enhanced surface tension via electrostatic contributions. Fork 4 (rigid geometry) uses linear angles (80°) and stiff force constants to test how conformational rigidity affects packing dynamics and thermodynamic properties.
hypothesis.expected_benefit: This parallel exploration will deconvolve the contributions of dispersive forces (Fork 2), electrostatic forces (Fork 3), and conformational constraints (Fork 4) to the target properties. By comparing these diverse strategies, we can identify whether the target polar liquid (high density, high dipole) is dominated by van der Waals or electrostatic interactions, guiding subsequent refinement toward optimal parameters.
hypothesis.test_method: The hypothesis will be tested by evaluating all 4 parameter sets in parallel MD simulations, comparing their ability to reproduce the target density, heat of vaporization, surface tension, and dipole moment at 298K and 323K.
